content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the branch diff against `origin/master`, read the changed CLI implementation and related tests end-to-end, and found one correctness gap in the deprecation notice coverage.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: `project create --from-requirements` does not emit the new deprecation notice. In `src/cli/project.rs:185-189`, the `Create` branch bypasses `handle_create(...)` and jumps straight to `handle_create_from_requirements(run_id)` when `--from-requirements` is present, and `src/cli/project.rs:267-316` never calls `print_deprecation_notice(...)`. That leaves one of the primary legacy `project create` entry points without the promised stderr warning, which violates the bead scope and acceptance criteria. The existing regression coverage for this path in `tests/cli.rs:6940-7009` only asserts success/stdout behavior, so the gap is currently untested. remediation_hint: emit the notice before branching on `from_requirements` or at the top of `handle_create_from_requirements`, and extend one of the `project_create_from_requirements_*` tests to assert the warning appears on stderr.
   Rationale: Users invoking the compatibility alias through the requirements-based flow currently receive no migration guidance at all, even though this is still a `project create` CLI entry point that the bead explicitly meant to deprecate consistently.
